chunk_summary: Standardizing growth references in tele-nutrition ensures consistent interpretation, prevents harm, and aligns remote anthropometry with WHO and IAP guidelines for children under 5 and 5–18 years respectively.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of consistent growth references in tele-nutrition services.
Learn how to apply WHO standards for children under 5 and IAP 2015 charts for ages 5–18.
Identify escalation thresholds for urgent referrals based on digital tool outputs.
Recognize the risks of misclassification using inappropriate growth references.
key_facts: WHO standards are recommended for children under 5 years, focusing on WAZ, HAZ, and WHZ indices.
IAP 2015 growth charts are used for children aged 5–18 years, emphasizing BAZ and height trajectory with pubertal context.
Urgent referral thresholds include WHZ below -3 or MUAC <115 mm for children aged 6–59 months.
Misclassification using adult BMI thresholds can lead to harmful interventions in children.
Growth faltering pathways should include predefined centile crossing thresholds for reassessment.
reference_quote: WHO standards for children under 5 years and IAP 2015 growth charts for ages 5–18 years provide a pragmatic approach to growth monitoring in Indian practice.
document_key_topics: WHO and IAP guidelines for pediatric growth monitoring
Escalation thresholds for malnutrition and growth faltering
